key_insight: A broad benchmark shows that neural-network and deep-learning surrogates predict wound-rotor synchronous motor torque and efficiency more accurately than tree-based models, with FT-Transformer performing best overall.
engineering_relevance: This enables much faster WRSM design-space exploration than finite element analysis, helping engineers optimize performance without expensive repeated simulations.
method: Generated 3,351 Motor-CAD samples using Latin Hypercube Sampling
Compared 8 models across 4 algorithmic families, including FT-Transformer
Used Optuna tuning and 10 random seeds for each model
evidence_signal: The study evaluated models on WRSM torque and efficiency using R², MAE, RMSE, and MAPE; FT-Transformer reached R² = 0.9928 and inference time of 0.33 ms.
watch_item: Surrogate accuracy may not transfer well outside the sampled design space, so extrapolation to unseen WRSM geometries or operating conditions should be treated cautiously.
paper_title: Comparative Evaluation of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models for Wound-Rotor Synchronous Motor Performance Prediction
